name: Kettlebell Lateral Raise
resistance_profile: Free-weight torque peaks around horizontal; the kettlebell's mass hangs offset below the hand, adding a rotational and grip demand and a slightly harder feel near the top.
cues: Lead with the elbow and let the bell hang
Slight forward lean to load the side delt
Control the bell — do not let it swing
execution_steps: Hold a kettlebell in each hand (or a single bell for single-arm work) with the bells hanging outside your thighs, arms at your sides.
Stand tall with a slight forward lean, soft knees, and a small bend in the elbows.
Lead with the elbows and raise the bells out to the sides to about shoulder height, letting each kettlebell rotate naturally against the forearm.
Pause briefly at the top without shrugging the traps.
Lower slowly under control back to the start and repeat.
good_when: Kettlebells are the only free weights available
You want extra grip and forearm demand alongside side-delt work
bad_when: The offset load aggravates the wrist or grip fails before the delts
You want the cleanest, most stable line of pull (a machine or cable is better)
common_errors: Swinging the bells up with momentum
Shrugging the traps to finish the rep
Letting the wrist collapse under the offset load
progressions: Add reps, then move to a heavier kettlebell
regressions: Switch to a dumbbell for a more stable load
Perform single-arm to halve the total load
